summary: This week's AI news highlights significant advancements in AI technology, including OpenAI's new family of models and Meta's AI-powered chips. However, concerns over AI ethics and regulation continue to grow, with lawsuits and sanctions looming over companies like OpenAI and Meta.
insight: The increasing use of AI in various industries is leading to a growing need for transparency and accountability, as seen in the lawsuits against OpenAI and Meta, highlighting the importance of responsible AI development and deployment.
highlights: OpenAI launches its new family of models with GPT-5.6, promising improvements in cybersecurity and other areas.
Meta is developing AI-powered chips, taking a modular approach to design, anticipating rapid changes in AI technology.
Lawsuits and sanctions are looming over OpenAI and Meta, highlighting concerns over AI ethics and regulation.

articles.title: Apple sues OpenAI for allegedly stealing hardware secrets
articles.description: Apple has sued OpenAI, alleging that engineers stole Apple secrets to advance the AI startup's hardware plans. In its complaint, Apple says it uncovered "a pattern of theft of Appl

articles.title: OpenAI launches its new family of models with GPT-5.6
articles.description: OpenAI's latest family of models promises improvements across a range of areas, including cybersecurity.

articles.title: OpenAI bets on families as ChatGPT goes deeper into households
articles.description: ChatGPT is hiring a dedicated product manager to build experiences for families, caregivers, and older adults, according to a job posting.

articles.title: Meta’s new AI chips will begin production in September
articles.description: The company is taking a modular approach to designing these chips, anticipating that their needs will change as AI evolves rapidly by the time the chips are in production.

articles.title: OpenAI says GPT 5.6 is the ‘preferred model’ for Microsoft Copilot 365 amid breakup chatter
articles.description: OpenAI's new family of models will continue to power Microsoft's suite of workplace and productivity apps.

articles.title: New York Times says OpenAI hid evidence in ChatGPT copyright trial
articles.description: News publishers say OpenAI hid tools and datasets that could identify copyrighted journalism in ChatGPT outputs, escalating their lawsuit with a new motion for sanctions.

articles.title: Apple’s failed self-driving car program left a legacy of powerful AI chips
articles.description: Apple's self-driving car program never really got off the ground, but it may have been what made the company's chips the powerful AI performers they are. Early in the development o

articles.title: Open source AI matters more than ever, according to Hugging Face’s Clem Delangue
articles.description: Open source AI is booming, according to Hugging Face CEO Clem Delangue. The company has grown into something like a GitHub for AI in recent years, where AI builders can share and d

articles.title: Humanoid robots controlled by surgeons did world-first operation on live pigs
articles.description: Preclinical trial is testing the feasibility of humanoid robots in surgery.

articles.title: GPT-5.6: Frontier intelligence that scales with your ambition
articles.description: More intelligence from every token, stronger performance per dollar, and more capability on demand for your hardest work.

articles.title: Disable autoplay and infinite scroll or risk massive fines, EU tells Meta
articles.description: Digital Services Act may force Meta to make big changes on its platforms.

articles.title: Lorde says Ray-Ban Meta AI glasses are ‘not sexy’
